title: AWS Certified AI Practitioner (AIF-C01)
description: Practice exam for AWS Certified AI Practitioner — 20 questions covering all 5 domains

domains.name: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML

lessons.title: Bài 1: AI, ML & Deep Learning Concepts
Bài 2: ML Lifecycle & AWS AI Services

questions.question: What is a Foundation Model?
options: A model designed for only one specific task
A large AI model pre-trained on broad data that can be adapted to many downstream tasks
A model that only processes structured tabular data
A model trained entirely using Reinforcement Learning
questions.explanation: A Foundation Model is a large AI model pre-trained on vast, diverse datasets. It can be adapted to many downstream tasks through fine-tuning, RAG, or prompt engineering.

questions.question: What is the PRIMARY purpose of Amazon Bedrock?
options: Accessing and using Foundation Models from multiple providers through a single API
questions.explanation: Amazon Bedrock is a fully managed service that provides access to Foundation Models from multiple providers (Anthropic, Meta, Amazon, Mistral, etc.) through a single API for building generative AI applications.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.question: How does Supervised Learning differ from Unsupervised Learning?
options: Supervised Learning does not require any data
Supervised Learning uses labeled data to train the model
Unsupervised Learning always produces more accurate results
Supervised Learning can only be used for classification tasks
questions.explanation: Supervised Learning uses labeled data (input-output pairs) to train models for classification or regression, while Unsupervised Learning discovers hidden patterns in unlabeled data (e.g., clustering).

questions.question: What problem does RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) solve for LLMs?
options: It reduces hallucination by grounding responses in external knowledge sources
It increases the context window size
questions.explanation: RAG combines retrieval of relevant external data with generation, helping LLMs produce more accurate, fact-based answers by grounding responses in retrieved documents rather than relying solely on training knowledge.

questions.question: A customer support chatbot gives inconsistent and overly creative answers to factual questions. Which inference parameter should be adjusted?
questions.explanation: Lower temperature values (closer to 0) make the model more deterministic and focused, producing consistent and factual responses. Higher temperature values increase randomness and creativity.

questions.question: Which prompting technique is MOST effective for improving a model's accuracy on complex mathematical reasoning tasks?
questions.explanation: Chain-of-Thought prompting instructs the model to reason step by step before giving a final answer. This significantly improves accuracy on math, logic, and multi-step reasoning tasks.

questions.question: A company wants to build a Q&A assistant that answers questions from internal documents stored in Amazon S3. The documents are updated weekly. Which approach is MOST suitable?
options: Fine-tune a foundation model on the documents
Use RAG with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases
Pre-train a custom model from scratch
Use zero-shot prompting with a large context window
questions.explanation: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases provides managed RAG — it automatically chunks, embeds, and indexes S3 documents, retrieves relevant information per query, and stays current via auto-sync without model retraining.

questions.question: Which Transformer architecture type is BEST suited for text generation tasks such as chatbots and content creation?
questions.explanation: Decoder-only architectures (like GPT, Claude, Llama) generate text autoregressively one token at a time and are the basis for most modern chatbots and text generators.

questions.question: A retail company wants to build an AI assistant that can check inventory, process returns, and answer product questions from their catalog. Which Amazon Bedrock feature should they use?
options: Bedrock Agents with Action Groups and Knowledge Bases
questions.explanation: Bedrock Agents can orchestrate multi-step tasks by calling APIs (action groups for inventory/returns) and retrieving information (knowledge bases for product catalog) — combining reasoning with actions.

questions.question: Which technique allows fine-tuning a large language model while updating only a small fraction of the model's parameters?
questions.explanation: LoRA is a Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) technique that adds small trainable adapter matrices while freezing the original model weights — typically updating less than 1% of total parameters, reducing cost significantly.

questions.question: What is the PRIMARY purpose of Amazon Bedrock Guardrails?
options: Implementing safety controls such as content filtering, denied topics, and PII detection for AI applications
questions.explanation: Bedrock Guardrails implement safety controls including content filters (hate, violence, sexual), denied topics, word filters, PII detection/redaction, and contextual grounding checks — applied to both model inputs and outputs.

questions.question: A hiring AI system consistently ranks male candidates higher than equally qualified female candidates. What is the MOST likely cause?
options: Selection bias in the training data reflecting historical hiring patterns
The model's architecture is too complex
The inference temperature is set too high
questions.explanation: If training data contained historical hiring decisions that favored male candidates, the model would learn and reproduce that selection bias — the training data didn't represent the qualified population fairly.

questions.question: Which AWS service can detect bias in ML model predictions and provide per-prediction explainability using SHAP values?
questions.explanation: SageMaker Clarify provides pre-training bias detection (data analysis), post-training bias detection (prediction analysis across demographic groups), and model explainability through SHAP values.

questions.question: A document processing application needs human review when AI-extracted data has low confidence. Which AWS service provides this human-in-the-loop capability?
questions.explanation: Amazon A2I provides human-in-the-loop workflows with built-in integration for Amazon Textract and Rekognition. It automatically triggers human review when AI confidence falls below a defined threshold.

questions.question: A financial services company wants to ensure Amazon Bedrock API calls do NOT traverse the public internet. What should they configure?
options: VPC endpoint (AWS PrivateLink) for Amazon Bedrock
questions.explanation: A VPC interface endpoint (AWS PrivateLink) for Amazon Bedrock allows private connectivity from within a VPC without any traffic going through the public internet.

questions.question: According to the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, who is responsible for ensuring ML training data does not contain bias?
options: Both AWS and the customer equally
questions.explanation: Under the Shared Responsibility Model, customers are responsible for 'security IN the cloud' — including training data quality, bias detection, model selection, IAM, and ethical AI practices.

questions.question: A chatbot must NEVER reveal customer credit card numbers in responses. Which approach provides the STRONGEST guarantee?
options: Add 'never output credit card numbers' to the system prompt
Fine-tune the model to avoid outputting PII
Use Amazon Bedrock Guardrails with PII filters set to BLOCK
Remove credit card numbers from the knowledge base
questions.explanation: Bedrock Guardrails with PII filters provide programmatic detection and blocking of credit card numbers — this cannot be bypassed by prompt injection, unlike system prompts which are soft constraints.

questions.question: A company needs to discover which Amazon S3 buckets contain personally identifiable information (PII) before using the data for ML training. Which AWS service should they use?
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie uses ML to automatically discover and classify sensitive data (including PII) stored in Amazon S3 buckets. Comprehend detects PII in text at runtime, but Macie is designed for S3-level data discovery.

questions.question: A company wants to process 50,000 customer reviews overnight using a foundation model for sentiment analysis. Which Amazon Bedrock pricing model is MOST cost-effective?
questions.explanation: Batch Inference is designed for large-scale, non-real-time workloads and offers up to 50% cost savings compared to on-demand pricing. Ideal for processing large datasets overnight.

questions.question: A non-technical marketing team wants to experiment with generative AI applications without an AWS account or coding skills. Which AWS service should they use?
questions.explanation: Amazon PartyRock is a free, no-code playground for generative AI that requires no AWS account. Users can build and share GenAI apps with drag-and-drop — ideal for experimentation and learning.

title: AWS Certified Machine Learning - Specialty
description: Luyện thi chứng chỉ AWS ML Specialty — build, train, deploy ML trên AWS

questions.question: SageMaker built-in algorithm nào phù hợp nhất cho bài toán phát hiện bất thường (anomaly detection)?
questions.explanation: Random Cut Forest (RCF) là thuật toán unsupervised trong SageMaker, chuyên detect anomaly trong dữ liệu streaming hoặc time series.

questions.question: Feature Store trong SageMaker dùng để làm gì?
options: Lưu trữ mô hình đã train
Quản lý và chia sẻ features giữa các team ML, đảm bảo consistency
questions.explanation: SageMaker Feature Store là kho lưu trữ features centralized, giúp các team ML chia sẻ features, tránh duplicate work, và đảm bảo tính nhất quán giữa training và inference.

questions.question: SageMaker sử dụng mode nào để train trên nhiều instance cùng lúc?
questions.explanation: SageMaker hỗ trợ distributed training cho phép chia workload training ra nhiều instances (data parallelism hoặc model parallelism) để tăng tốc.

questions.question: SageMaker Model Monitor phát hiện loại drift nào?
options: Data quality, model quality, bias drift, và feature attribution drift
questions.explanation: SageMaker Model Monitor phát hiện 4 loại: Data Quality (thay đổi schema/statistics), Model Quality (độ chính xác giảm), Bias Drift (bias thay đổi), Feature Attribution Drift (feature importance thay đổi).

questions.question: Khi nào nên dùng SageMaker Inference Pipeline?
options: Khi cần chain nhiều bước xử lý (preprocessing → model → postprocessing) trong một endpoint
questions.explanation: Inference Pipeline cho phép chain tối đa 15 containers trong một endpoint — ví dụ: data preprocessing → feature engineering → model prediction → postprocessing.

questions.question: BlazingText trong SageMaker được dùng cho tác vụ nào?
questions.explanation: BlazingText là implementation siêu nhanh của Word2Vec và nhận diện text classification. Nó hỗ trợ training trên multi-GPU với tốc độ rất cao.

questions.question: SageMaker Ground Truth được sử dụng để?
options: Tạo labeled datasets với hỗ trợ của human annotators và active learning
questions.explanation: Ground Truth là dịch vụ data labeling, kết hợp human annotators (Amazon Mechanical Turk, private team, hoặc vendors) với active learning để giảm chi phí labeling.

questions.question: Elastic Inference trong SageMaker dùng để?
options: Gắn GPU fractional vào instance để giảm chi phí inference
Tự động scale số lượng model
questions.explanation: Elastic Inference cho phép gắn GPU acceleration với chi phí thấp vào SageMaker endpoints hoặc notebook instances — chỉ trả tiền cho GPU resource thực sự dùng.

questions.question: Chiến lược nào giúp xử lý dữ liệu mất cân bằng (imbalanced dataset)?
options: SMOTE (oversampling), undersampling, class weights, hoặc ensemble methods
questions.explanation: Imbalanced data cần kỹ thuật đặc biệt: SMOTE tạo thêm sample cho class thiểu số, undersampling giảm class đa số, hoặc điều chỉnh class weights trong loss function.

questions.question: SageMaker Clarify dùng để?
options: Phát hiện bias trong dữ liệu và mô hình, giải thích dự đoán (explainability)
questions.explanation: SageMaker Clarify giúp phát hiện bias trong data và model, cung cấp feature importance (SHAP values), hỗ trợ Responsible AI và regulatory compliance.

questions.question: DeepAR trong SageMaker được dùng cho bài toán nào?
options: Dự báo chuỗi thời gian (time series forecasting)
questions.explanation: DeepAR là thuật toán RNN-based cho time series forecasting, đặc biệt hiệu quả khi có nhiều chuỗi thời gian liên quan (cold-start problem).

questions.question: Multi-Model Endpoint trong SageMaker có ưu điểm gì?
options: Host nhiều model trên cùng một endpoint, giảm chi phí khi có nhiều model ít traffic
questions.explanation: Multi-Model Endpoint cho phép host hàng trăm model trên cùng endpoint, load model on-demand — tiết kiệm chi phí rất lớn so với mỗi model một endpoint riêng.

questions.question: Khi nào nên dùng SageMaker Batch Transform thay vì Real-time Endpoint?
options: Khi cần xử lý inference cho dataset lớn không cần response ngay lập tức
questions.explanation: Batch Transform phù hợp khi cần inference lượng lớn dữ liệu, không cần response real-time — ví dụ: nightly scoring, preprocessing dataset lớn.

questions.question: SageMaker Autopilot là gì?
options: AutoML — tự động phân tích data, thử nhiều algorithms, và chọn model tốt nhất
questions.explanation: SageMaker Autopilot là giải pháp AutoML, tự động phân tích data, feature engineering, thử nhiều algorithms/hyperparameters, và đề xuất model tốt nhất — kèm notebook giải thích.

questions.question: Pipe Mode trong SageMaker training có lợi ích gì?
options: Stream dữ liệu trực tiếp từ S3 vào training container, không cần download toàn bộ trước
questions.explanation: Pipe Mode stream dữ liệu từ S3 vào training container thay vì copy toàn bộ (File Mode) — giảm startup time và disk requirement, đặc biệt hiệu quả với dataset lớn.

title: Google Cloud Professional ML Engineer
description: Practice exam for Google Cloud Professional Machine Learning Engineer — 50 questions covering all domains

questions.question: Vertex AI Pipeline được xây dựng trên framework nào?
questions.explanation: Vertex AI Pipelines dựa trên Kubeflow Pipelines SDK và TFX (TensorFlow Extended), cho phép orchestrate ML workflow trên Google Cloud.

questions.question: BigQuery ML cho phép làm gì đặc biệt?
options: Train và deploy ML model trực tiếp bằng SQL trong BigQuery
Chỉ export dữ liệu sang CSV
questions.explanation: BigQuery ML (BQML) cho phép data analysts train model ML bằng SQL quen thuộc ngay trong BigQuery — không cần viết Python hay setup infrastructure riêng.

questions.question: Vertex AI Feature Store khác gì so với lưu features trong database thông thường?
options: Hỗ trợ serving features với low-latency, đảm bảo training-serving consistency, và feature versioning
questions.explanation: Feature Store chuyên biệt cho ML: serving features online (low-latency) và offline (batch), đảm bảo features đồng nhất giữa training và serving, hỗ trợ time-travel và monitoring.

questions.question: Khi nào nên dùng AutoML thay vì custom training trên Vertex AI?
options: Khi cần kiểm soát hoàn toàn architecture
Khi team không có nhiều ML expertise hoặc cần baseline model nhanh
questions.explanation: AutoML phù hợp khi cần model nhanh, team có ít ML expertise, hoặc cần baseline. Custom training khi cần kiểm soát architecture, thuật toán đặc thù, hoặc tối ưu sâu.

questions.question: Vertex AI Experiments dùng để?
options: Track, compare và reproduce ML experiments (hyperparameters, metrics, artifacts)
questions.explanation: Vertex AI Experiments cung cấp experiment tracking: log hyperparameters, metrics, model artifacts — cho phép compare nhiều runs và reproduce kết quả.

questions.question: TFX (TensorFlow Extended) bao gồm những component chính nào?
options: ExampleGen, StatisticsGen, SchemaGen, ExampleValidator, Transform, Trainer, Evaluator, Pusher
questions.explanation: TFX là end-to-end ML platform gồm: ExampleGen (ingest), StatisticsGen + SchemaGen + ExampleValidator (validate), Transform (feature eng), Trainer, Tuner, Evaluator, Pusher (deploy).

questions.question: Vertex AI Model Monitoring kiểm tra điều gì?
options: Skew (training-serving) và drift (prediction data thay đổi theo thời gian)
questions.explanation: Model Monitoring phát hiện: training-serving skew (feature distribution khác nhau) và prediction drift (dữ liệu production drift khỏi baseline), trigger alert khi vượt threshold.

questions.question: Google Cloud AI Platform Prediction hỗ trợ chiến lược deploy nào?
options: Traffic splitting cho A/B testing và canary deployments
questions.explanation: Vertex AI Prediction hỗ trợ traffic splitting: có thể route % traffic sang model versions khác nhau — phục vụ A/B testing, canary release, và progressive rollout.

questions.question: Dataflow trong ML pipeline đóng vai trò gì?
options: Xử lý dữ liệu quy mô lớn (batch & streaming) cho data preprocessing/feature engineering
questions.explanation: Dataflow (dựa trên Apache Beam) xử lý data ở scale lớn: ETL, feature engineering cho cả batch và streaming — bước tiền xử lý quan trọng trong ML pipeline.

questions.question: Vertex AI Matching Engine dùng cho bài toán nào?
options: Tìm kiếm nearest neighbor (vector similarity search) ở quy mô lớn
questions.explanation: Matching Engine là managed approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) service — dùng cho similarity search, recommendation, RAG retrieval ở quy mô tỷ vectors.

questions.question: Vertex AI Workbench khác gì Colab Enterprise?
options: Workbench là JupyterLab managed instances cho ML production, Colab Enterprise cho collaboration và exploration
Colab Enterprise chỉ dùng miễn phí
questions.explanation: Workbench cung cấp JupyterLab managed instances với tích hợp sâu vào GCP services (BigQuery, GCS) cho production ML. Colab Enterprise thiên về collaboration, sharing và exploration.

questions.question: Kỹ thuật nào giảm kích thước model để deploy trên edge devices?
questions.explanation: Model compression: Quantization (giảm precision: FP32→INT8), Pruning (loại bỏ weights/neurons không quan trọng), Knowledge Distillation (teacher model dạy student model nhỏ hơn).

questions.question: Vertex AI GenAI Studio dùng để?
options: Prototyping, testing, và tuning Foundation Models (PaLM, Gemini) trên Google Cloud
questions.explanation: GenAI Studio cung cấp UI và API để thử nghiệm Foundation Models, prompt design, tuning, và deploy — không cần ML expertise sâu.

questions.question: Khi data có nhiều missing values, chiến lược nào phù hợp?
options: Luôn xoá rows có missing values
Tuỳ context: imputation (mean/median/mode, KNN, model-based), hoặc tạo indicator feature cho missingness
Bỏ qua và train trực tiếp
questions.explanation: Xử lý missing values tuỳ thuộc vào pattern (MCAR/MAR/MNAR): imputation thống kê (mean/median), model-based (KNN, MICE), hoặc thêm feature indicator. Xoá rows chỉ khi missing ít và MCAR.

questions.question: Continuous Training (CT) trong MLOps là gì?
options: Tự động retrain model khi phát hiện trigger (data drift, schedule, hoặc performance degradation)
Train model thủ công hàng tuần
questions.explanation: Continuous Training tự động kích hoạt retrain pipeline khi: data mới đến (scheduled), data drift vượt threshold, hoặc model performance giảm — đảm bảo model luôn fresh.

title: KCNA — Kubernetes and Cloud Native Associate
description: Practice exam for KCNA — 20 multiple-choice questions covering all 5 domains

lessons.title: Bài 7: Cloud Native Architecture & Design Patterns
Bài 9: Application Delivery — Helm, GitOps & CI/CD

questions.question: Which Kubernetes component is responsible for watching for newly created Pods with no assigned node and selecting a node for them to run on?
questions.explanation: kube-scheduler watches for newly created Pods and selects the best node for them based on resource availability, constraints, and policies.

questions.question: Which component stores all cluster state in Kubernetes?
questions.explanation: etcd is the consistent and highly-available key-value store used as Kubernetes' backing store for all cluster state.

questions.question: A Pod is in 'Pending' state. What is the MOST likely reason?
options: No node satisfies the scheduling requirements
questions.explanation: Pending means the Pod has been accepted but one or more containers have not been started — most commonly because no node has sufficient resources or the Pod doesn't match any node's constraints.

questions.question: Which Kubernetes resource ensures exactly one Pod runs on every node in the cluster?
questions.explanation: A DaemonSet ensures that all (or some) Nodes run a copy of a Pod — commonly used for log collectors, monitoring agents, and network plugins.

questions.question: What is the default Service type in Kubernetes?
questions.explanation: ClusterIP is the default Service type. It exposes the Service on a cluster-internal IP, making it only accessible from within the cluster.

questions.question: Which access mode allows a PersistentVolume to be mounted as read-write by many nodes simultaneously?
questions.explanation: ReadWriteMany (RWX) allows the volume to be mounted as read-write by many nodes simultaneously, required for shared storage scenarios.

questions.question: Which RBAC resource grants permissions within a specific namespace?
questions.explanation: Role is namespace-scoped. ClusterRole is cluster-wide. A Role combined with a RoleBinding grants permissions within a specific namespace.

questions.question: In a StatefulSet, what is the naming pattern for Pods?
questions.explanation: StatefulSet Pods have a stable, unique ordinal identity (web-0, web-1, web-2). This predictable naming enables ordered deployment, scaling, and rolling updates.

questions.question: Which is the default container runtime interface (CRI) used by Kubernetes since v1.20?
questions.explanation: containerd is the default CRI since Kubernetes deprecated Dockershim in v1.20. containerd is lightweight and directly implements the CRI specification.

questions.question: What does OCI stand for in the context of containers?
questions.explanation: OCI stands for Open Container Initiative — an open governance structure for creating open industry standards around container formats and runtimes.

questions.question: Which Kubernetes feature automatically scales the number of Pod replicas based on CPU utilization?
questions.explanation: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) automatically scales the number of Pod replicas based on observed CPU utilization or custom metrics.

questions.question: According to the 12-factor app methodology, how should application configuration be stored?
options: In config files committed to git
questions.explanation: Factor III of 12-factor app: Store config in the environment. Configuration that varies between deployments should be stored in environment variables, not in code.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Service Mesh in a microservices architecture?
options: Managing service-to-service communication, observability, and security
questions.explanation: A service mesh (like Istio or Linkerd) handles service-to-service communication with features like load balancing, mTLS encryption, circuit breaking, observability, and traffic management.

questions.question: Which CNCF project is the primary service mesh used in production Kubernetes environments?
questions.explanation: Istio is the most widely adopted service mesh. It provides traffic management, security (mTLS), and observability via an Envoy sidecar proxy injected into each Pod.

questions.question: What differentiates serverless computing from traditional container deployments?
options: Serverless abstracts server management; scales to zero when idle
Serverless is only for batch workloads
questions.explanation: Serverless abstracts infrastructure management — the platform handles scaling, including scaling to zero when there's no traffic, so you pay only for actual execution time.

questions.question: Which is the standard CNCF observability data format that unifies metrics, logs, and traces?
questions.explanation: OpenTelemetry (OTel) is the CNCF standard for observability — providing a single set of APIs, SDKs, and tools to collect, process, and export metrics, logs, and traces.

questions.question: In the SLO/SLI/SLA framework, what does SLI stand for?
questions.explanation: SLI (Service Level Indicator) is a quantitative measure of service behavior. Examples: request latency, error rate, throughput. SLOs are targets based on SLIs.

questions.question: What is GitOps?
options: A practice where Git is the single source of truth for declarative infrastructure and code
questions.explanation: GitOps uses Git repositories as the single source of truth for declarative infrastructure. Automated agents (like Argo CD or Flux) reconcile the actual state with the desired state in Git.

questions.question: Which command installs a Helm chart with the release name 'myapp' from a chart directory?
questions.explanation: helm install <release-name> <chart-path> is the correct command. You can also use --values to override defaults and --namespace to specify the target namespace.

questions.question: In a canary deployment strategy, what is the purpose of the 'canary' version?
options: To replace the stable version immediately
To test the new version with a small percentage of traffic before full rollout
To run only in staging environment
questions.explanation: Canary deployment sends a small percentage of traffic (e.g., 5-10%) to the new version. If metrics are healthy, traffic is gradually increased. This limits blast radius of new version issues.

title: CKA — Certified Kubernetes Administrator
description: Practice exam for CKA — 20 scenario-based questions covering all 5 domains

questions.question: You need to backup etcd on the control plane node. The etcd data directory is /var/lib/etcd, CA cert at /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt, server cert at /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt, and key at /etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key. Which command creates the snapshot?
questions.explanation: ETCDCTL_API=3 sets the API version. The etcdctl snapshot save command requires --cacert, --cert, and --key for authentication. Without ETCDCTL_API=3, etcdctl defaults to v2 which doesn't support snapshot.

questions.question: A cluster has Kubernetes v1.30 and you need to upgrade it to v1.31. What is the correct order of operations?
options: Upgrade worker nodes first, then control plane
Upgrade all nodes simultaneously with kubeadm upgrade apply
Upgrade control plane first, then drain and upgrade each worker node
Upgrade etcd first, then control plane, then workers
questions.explanation: Always upgrade the control plane first (kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.x), then upgrade each worker node one at a time: drain the node, upgrade kubeadm/kubelet/kubectl, then uncordon.

questions.question: You need to create a ClusterRole 'pod-reader' that allows get, watch, list on pods, then bind it to user 'jane'. Which commands achieve this?
options: kubectl create role pod-reader --verb=get,watch,list --resource=pods && kubectl create rolebinding pod-reader --role=pod-reader --user=jane
kubectl create clusterrole pod-reader --verb=get,watch,list --resource=pods && kubectl create clusterrolebinding pod-reader --clusterrole=pod-reader --user=jane
questions.explanation: ClusterRole is cluster-scoped. ClusterRoleBinding binds a ClusterRole to a user cluster-wide. If you used Role + RoleBinding, jane would only have access in one namespace.

questions.question: A node has a taint 'key=value:NoSchedule'. How do you allow a Pod to be scheduled on this node?
options: Add a nodeSelector: {key: value} to the Pod spec
Add a toleration to the Pod spec: {key: key, operator: Equal, value: value, effect: NoSchedule}
questions.explanation: Tolerations allow Pods to be scheduled on nodes with matching taints. The toleration must match the taint key, value, and effect. Without the toleration, the scheduler will skip this node.

questions.question: You want to ensure a Pod is ONLY scheduled on nodes labeled 'disktype=ssd'. Which scheduling feature should you use?
options: nodeSelector is sufficient for strict requirements
questions.explanation: Node Affinity with requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution enforces strict node selection. nodeSelector works too, but Node Affinity provides richer expressions (In, NotIn, Exists, etc.).

questions.question: A Pod cannot resolve the DNS name 'my-service.default.svc.cluster.local'. CoreDNS pods are running. What should you check FIRST?
options: Check if the Service exists and its selector matches the Pod labels
Restart the kubelet on all nodes
Check if the Pod has network access
questions.explanation: First verify the Service exists (kubectl get svc my-service) and its selector matches the target Pods (kubectl get endpoints my-service). If Endpoints are empty, the selector doesn't match any Pods.

questions.question: You need to create a NetworkPolicy that denies ALL ingress traffic to Pods in namespace 'prod'. Which spec achieves this?
questions.explanation: An empty podSelector ({}) selects ALL Pods in the namespace. Specifying policyTypes: [Ingress] without any ingress rules creates a default deny for all ingress traffic.

questions.question: A PersistentVolumeClaim is stuck in 'Pending' state. What is the MOST likely cause?
options: The Pod using the PVC is not running
No PersistentVolume matches the PVC's storage class, access mode, or capacity requirements
kubectl was not used to create the PVC
questions.explanation: PVC Pending means no PV matched the binding criteria: storageClassName, accessModes, and requested storage capacity. Check with kubectl describe pvc to see the binding failure reason.

questions.question: Which reclaim policy ensures a PersistentVolume is deleted when its claim is released?
questions.explanation: Delete policy automatically deletes the PV and its associated storage asset when the PVC is deleted. Retain keeps the PV data for manual recovery. Recycle is deprecated.

questions.question: A Node shows status 'NotReady'. Which command gives you the MOST useful diagnostic information?
options: kubectl describe node <node-name> and check Events/Conditions sections
questions.explanation: kubectl describe node shows Conditions (DiskPressure, MemoryPressure, NetworkUnavailable, Ready), resource usage, and Events. On the node itself, check: systemctl status kubelet and journalctl -u kubelet.

questions.question: A Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff'. Which command shows the logs from the PREVIOUS container instance?
questions.explanation: kubectl logs --previous (or -p) shows the logs from the previously terminated container instance, which is crucial for diagnosing why a container crashed.

questions.question: A Pod is stuck in 'ImagePullBackOff'. What is the MOST common cause?
options: Insufficient CPU resources on the node
Wrong image name/tag or missing image pull secret for private registry
questions.explanation: ImagePullBackOff is caused by: (1) incorrect image name/tag, (2) missing or incorrect imagePullSecret for private registries, (3) registry is unreachable. Check kubectl describe pod for the exact error.

questions.question: After upgrading a cluster, several Pods show 'OOMKilled'. What is the immediate remediation?
options: Increase the Pod's memory limit or add more nodes to the cluster
questions.explanation: OOMKilled means the container exceeded its memory limit. Fix: increase the memory limit in the Pod spec, or add more nodes if the node is under memory pressure. Also investigate for memory leaks.

questions.question: How do you run a temporary debug pod in a namespace to test network connectivity?
questions.explanation: kubectl run with --rm (auto-delete) and -it (interactive terminal) creates a temporary Pod for debugging. The -- sh runs a shell. Use --restart=Never to prevent it from being rescheduled.

questions.question: Which file does kubeadm use to store cluster configuration after initialization?
questions.explanation: kubeadm stores its configuration in a ConfigMap named 'kubeadm-config' in the kube-system namespace. This is used during upgrades: kubeadm reads this config with kubeadm upgrade plan.

questions.question: You need to deploy an app with the guarantee that it will NEVER be evicted under memory pressure. Which QoS class achieves this?
questions.explanation: Guaranteed QoS requires memory requests == memory limits (and CPU too). These Pods are evicted last. BestEffort (no requests/limits) is evicted first. Burstable is in between.

questions.question: Which command quickly tests if kube-proxy iptables rules are working for a Service named 'web' in the default namespace?
questions.explanation: Running curl from inside a Pod against the Service DNS name tests the full networking path: DNS resolution → kube-proxy IP tables → Pod endpoint. If DNS resolves but curl fails, it may be a kube-proxy or NetworkPolicy issue.

questions.question: A StatefulSet needs each Pod to have its own PersistentVolumeClaim. How is this configured?
options: Create individual PVCs manually and reference them by name in each Pod
Use volumeClaimTemplates in the StatefulSet spec
questions.explanation: volumeClaimTemplates in a StatefulSet spec automatically creates a PVC for each Pod replica. The PVC name follows the pattern <template-name>-<pod-name>. These PVCs are NOT deleted when the StatefulSet is deleted.

questions.question: What happens to running Pods when a node is drained with 'kubectl drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets'?
options: Pods are deleted immediately without rescheduling
Pods are evicted and rescheduled on other available nodes
Pods continue running but no new Pods are scheduled
Pods are paused until the node is uncordoned
questions.explanation: kubectl drain cordons the node (prevents new scheduling) then gracefully evicts existing Pods. The Pod controllers (Deployments, etc.) reschedule the Pods on other nodes. DaemonSet Pods are ignored with the flag.

questions.question: A Deployment has 3 replicas but only 1 Pod is running. The other 2 are Pending. What should you check?
options: Check if the Deployment manifest has errors
Check node resources (kubectl describe node), Pod events, and any PodDisruptionBudgets
questions.explanation: Pending Pods mean the scheduler can't place them. Check: (1) kubectl describe pod for Events (Insufficient CPU/memory, taint not tolerated), (2) kubectl describe node for available capacity, (3) any resource quotas blocking scheduling.

title: CKAD — Certified Kubernetes Application Developer
description: Practice exam for CKAD — 20 scenario-based questions covering all 5 domains

questions.question: A Pod needs to wait for a database to be ready before starting the main application container. Which Kubernetes feature should you use?
questions.explanation: Init containers run to completion before app containers start. Use an init container to check database connectivity (e.g., nslookup or nc -z db-host 5432). Only after it succeeds will the main container start.

questions.question: Which sidecar pattern is used to translate the communication protocol of the main container to match external services?
questions.explanation: The Adapter pattern transforms the output of the main container to match a standard format expected by external systems (e.g., converting proprietary metrics to Prometheus format). Ambassador is for proxying outbound connections.

questions.question: You need to run a batch job that should retry up to 4 times on failure. Which Job spec fields control this?
questions.explanation: backoffLimit specifies the number of retries before considering a Job failed. Default is 6. The restartPolicy should be Never or OnFailure. completions controls how many successful completions are needed.

questions.question: A Deployment is performing a rolling update. You spot a bug and need to stop it and go back to the previous version. Which commands achieve this?
options: kubectl delete deployment <name> && kubectl apply -f old-deployment.yaml
kubectl rollout pause <deployment> && kubectl rollout undo deployment/<name>
questions.explanation: kubectl rollout undo deployment/<name> reverts to the previous revision. You can also specify --to-revision=N to go to a specific version. Check history with kubectl rollout history deployment/<name>.

questions.question: In a RollingUpdate Deployment strategy, what does 'maxUnavailable: 1' mean?
options: Maximum 1 Pod can be created above the desired replicas during update
Maximum 1 Pod can be unavailable at any time during the update
The update fails if more than 1 Pod is unavailable
Maximum 1 node can be updated at a time
questions.explanation: maxUnavailable controls how many Pods can be down below the desired count during rolling updates. maxSurge controls how many extra Pods can be created above desired. These balance update speed vs availability.

questions.question: A container is running but not serving traffic. The Pod is Ready=False. Which probe is MOST likely failing?
questions.explanation: Readiness probe determines if a container is ready to accept traffic. A failing readiness probe removes the Pod from Service endpoints. Liveness probe triggers container restart. Startup probe guards slow-starting containers.

questions.question: A container keeps restarting. The liveness probe is configured with initialDelaySeconds: 5 but the app takes 30s to start. What is the fix?
options: Use a startup probe with failureThreshold * periodSeconds > 30s, or increase initialDelaySeconds to 35
questions.explanation: Use a startup probe: it protects slow-starting containers by allowing more time only at startup. Once the startup probe succeeds, the liveness probe takes over. Alternatively, initialDelaySeconds >= 35 would also work but startup probe is cleaner.

questions.question: You have a Secret named 'db-secret' with key 'password'. How do you inject it as an environment variable 'DB_PASSWORD' in a Pod?
options: env: [{name: DB_PASSWORD, valueFrom: {secretKeyRef: {name: db-secret, key: password}}}]
questions.explanation: valueFrom.secretKeyRef injects a specific key from a Secret as an env var. envFrom.secretRef injects ALL keys from the Secret as env vars. The first creates DB_PASSWORD specifically from the Secret's 'password' key.

questions.question: A container must run as user ID 1000 and cannot write to the root filesystem. Which SecurityContext settings achieve this?
questions.explanation: runAsUser: 1000 sets the UID. readOnlyRootFilesystem: true prevents any writes to the container filesystem (use volume mounts for writable paths). These are container-level SecurityContext fields.

questions.question: Which Pod Security Standard level prevents privilege escalation and restricts capabilities but is less restrictive than Restricted?
questions.explanation: Baseline prevents known privilege escalations while allowing the default Docker/container configuration. Restricted is the most hardened. Privileged allows everything. Apply with: kubectl label namespace <ns> pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=baseline

questions.question: A Pod consumes 500m CPU but has no limit set. Which QoS class is assigned?
questions.explanation: Burstable: at least one container has CPU or memory request/limit set, but not all (or requests != limits). BestEffort: no requests or limits at all. Guaranteed: all containers have requests == limits for both CPU and memory.

questions.question: You need to expose a Deployment externally on port 80 and route to container port 8080. Which Service spec is correct?
questions.explanation: LoadBalancer exposes the Service externally. port: 80 is the Service port that clients connect to. targetPort: 8080 is the container port traffic is forwarded to. NodePort would also work but exposes a random high port.

questions.question: An Ingress resource is created but returns 404 for all paths. What should you check FIRST?
options: Whether the Service backend exists and has endpoints
Whether the node has enough CPU
Whether the Ingress controller is installed and running
questions.explanation: Without an Ingress controller, Ingress resources have no effect. Check: kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx (or your controller namespace). Then verify the IngressClass matches your Ingress spec's ingressClassName.

questions.question: You need to create a NetworkPolicy that allows only Pods with label 'role=frontend' to access Pods with label 'role=backend' on port 5432. Which spec is correct?
options: All traffic is allowed by default; no NetworkPolicy needed
Create two NetworkPolicies: one for ingress on backend, one for egress on frontend
questions.explanation: The NetworkPolicy selects backend Pods (podSelector), then defines ingress rules allowing traffic from frontend Pods on port 5432. This is applied in the same namespace as the backend Pods.

questions.question: A CronJob schedule '0 2 * * *' will run the job at what time?
questions.explanation: Cron format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week. '0 2 * * *' = minute 0, hour 2, any day/month/weekday = 2:00 AM daily. Use crontab.guru to verify cron expressions.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'helm upgrade' and 'helm upgrade --install'?
options: No difference, both commands are identical
--install creates the release if it doesn't exist, upgrade only updates existing releases
--install skips the upgrade and only installs fresh
--install uses the latest chart version automatically
questions.explanation: helm upgrade requires the release to already exist. helm upgrade --install is idempotent: it installs if the release doesn't exist, upgrades if it does. This is ideal for CI/CD pipelines.

questions.question: A ConfigMap key 'app.conf' contains a multi-line configuration file. How do you mount it in a Pod at /etc/app/app.conf?
options: Use a volume with configMap.items to map the key to a specific path, then volumeMount at /etc/app
Copy the file into the container image
questions.explanation: Mount a ConfigMap as a volume: volumes[].configMap.items maps specific keys to paths. Then volumeMounts mounts at /etc/app. The file will appear at /etc/app/app.conf. env injection is for env vars, not files.

questions.question: You want to check resource usage of Pods in namespace 'production'. Which command shows CPU and memory consumption?
questions.explanation: kubectl top pod shows current CPU and memory usage per Pod (requires metrics-server). kubectl describe shows limits/requests but not actual usage. kubectl top node shows node-level usage.

questions.question: Two containers in the same Pod need to share data in memory (not persisted to disk). Which volume type achieves this?
questions.explanation: emptyDir with medium: Memory creates a tmpfs (RAM-backed) volume. It's shared between all containers in the Pod and disappears when the Pod is removed. Default emptyDir uses disk storage.

questions.question: You need a Service that doesn't have a cluster IP and returns the IPs of individual Pods directly. Which Service type achieves this?
questions.explanation: A Headless Service (clusterIP: None) skips kube-proxy and returns Pod IPs directly via DNS. Used by StatefulSets for stable network identities and by clients that need direct Pod connections (e.g., databases, Cassandra).

title: NVIDIA DLI — Generative AI with Diffusion Models & LLMs
description: Practice exam covering all 4 DLI GenAI courses — Diffusion Models, RAG Agents, Agentic AI, LLM Eval & Fine-tuning. 20 questions, coding-style.
